name: Buckley v. Valeo
question: Did the limits placed on electoral expenditures by the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971, and related provisions of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954, violate the First Amendment's freedom of speech and association clauses?
description: A case in which the Court both upheld and struck down portions of the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971. Restrictions on individual donations were not a violation of the donators' First Amendment rights; but certain restrictions of campaign expenditures did violate the First Amendment.
